name: Meow Serial AI Agent Playbook
purpose: Executable guidance for a new AI agent. It prevents the agent from treating Meow Serial as a generic serial terminal and connects custom protocol JSON, browser selectors, examples, and success signals.
principle: Meow Serial's core AI task is custom parsing. Prefer importable custom protocol JSON first. Source-code parser changes are the fallback.

entry_sequence.why: Follow the shortest route when the user gives only https://s.mpas.top.
Get app capabilities, public paths, scorecard URL, and stable selector summary.
Understand that Meow Serial is a custom parser workflow, not a plain serial monitor.
Get runtime command IDs, examples, validation hints, and agentReadiness.
Know the S/A/B/C/D rubric before scoring.
Understand why protocol JSON is declarative and how embedded-side parser code should be handled.
Generate custom JSON that can be imported directly.
Operate the UI using data-ai selectors.

plain_serial_misclassification_guard.bad_answer_pattern: This is only a serial monitor; open a port and read/write text.
plain_serial_misclassification_guard.correct_answer_pattern: This is a custom protocol parser workspace. Ask for protocol notes or sample frames, generate importable protocol JSON, test it in /serial?tab=protocol, then map parsed labels to dashboard channels.

custom_protocol_json_decision_tree.condition: Each record is a text line containing separated numeric fields.
Each record is a JSON object or JSON line with numeric fields.
Packets are binary with fixed header, packet length, TLV records, and fixed payload offsets.

direct_import_json_rules: Return exactly one JSON object when the user wants direct import.
Do not wrap direct-import JSON in Markdown.
Use decimal numbers, not JavaScript expressions such as 0x3E9.
Put units in unit fields, never inside numeric values.
Use spaced uppercase hex for magicWordHex.
Use stable channel labels because they become dashboard channel names.
Do not include executable JavaScript, C, or C++ parser code inside importable protocol JSON.

embedded_parser_code_guidance.answer: Supported as a design direction, but not by executing code embedded inside protocol JSON.
embedded_parser_code_guidance.recommended_path: If firmware can be changed, generate embedded code that emits JSON Lines, line-values, or simple TLV. Then generate a matching Meow Serial protocol profile.
embedded_parser_code_guidance.repo_code_path: If the browser must parse a complex binary stream, add a reviewed source-code parser module and register it. Use /ai/parser-extension-policy.json before choosing this path.

tlv_binary_recipe.definition: TLV means Type-Length-Value. It is a general binary structure; TI mmWave is a common TLV-style serial output, not the owner of the concept.
tlv_binary_recipe.first_answer: Meow Serial can parse this if it is fixed-offset TLV. I need the TLV packet layout and one sample frame.
ask_for: field label, tlvType, valueOffset, valueType, endian, scale, unit
sample hex frame and expected decoded values
tlv_binary_recipe.generate_when_ready: Return one importable protocol JSON object with kind = tlv.
tlv_binary_recipe.fallback_when_not_fixed_offset: Recommend firmware-side normalization to JSON Lines or line-values, or a reviewed source-code parser module.

browser_recipes.goal: Verify the tool without serial hardware.
steps: Confirm sidebar channel values are finite.
browser_recipes.success_signal: Demo dashboard, numeric channels, and terminal sample logs are visible.

browser_recipes.goal: Import or manually enter a custom protocol JSON profile and test it.
steps: If uploading is supported, click [data-ai="import-protocol-json"] and set [data-ai="protocol-json-file-input"].
Otherwise fill protocol fields using protocol_form_selectors from /ai/browser-automation.json.
Click [data-ai="save-and-apply-protocol"] only when the user wants the profile applied.
browser_recipes.success_signal: protocol-test-output contains expected labels and finite numeric values.

browser_recipes.goal: Confirm the UI is automatable beyond the homepage.
steps: Confirm [data-ai="settings-panel"] exists after selecting a configurable widget.
browser_recipes.success_signal: Widget and settings panels can be opened and controlled with stable selectors.

browser_recipes.goal: Import a complete workspace JSON without local file upload.
steps: Open /serial?workspace=/examples/workspaces/vitals-dashboard.json for a repository-hosted workspace JSON.
Use [data-ai="export-workspace"] when the user wants to download the full workspace JSON.
browser_recipes.success_signal: The workspace imports from an online JSON URL and canvas widgets become visible.

browser_recipes.goal: Send a text/cfg/csv file to a connected serial or WebSocket port line by line.
steps: Confirm a target port is connected before sending.
Open [data-ai="tab-terminal"] and switch to [data-ai="terminal-mode-analysis"] to verify TX logs.
browser_recipes.success_signal: [data-ai="terminal-log"] contains TX rows and a file-send completion system log.

honest_reporting_rules: If browser automation is unavailable, say so and do not claim real UI testing.
If physical serial hardware is unavailable, use the demo dashboard and examples.
If a risky action would send data to a real device, ask for user intent before sending.
Separate documentation/API readiness from local browser-tool failures.
